channel.title: US Top News and Analysis
channel.description: CNBC is the world leader in business news and real-time financial market coverage. Find fast, actionable information.

item.title: Trump says Iran deal could be reached in 'two or three days' and Strait of Hormuz will reopen 'immediately'
item.description: Speaking to reporters after attending the NBA Finals in New York, Trump said the two parties are in the final stages of a "very, very good deal".

item.title: OpenAI confidentially files for IPO, prepping Wall Street for mega AI debut
item.description: OpenAI's confidential filing lands days before SpaceX is set to go public and a week after Anthropic announced its confidential disclosure with the SEC.

item.title: WWDC 2026: Apple makes its big Siri AI reveal, changes Liquid Glass and more
item.description: Follow Apple WWDC 2026 live updates as the keynote spotlights AI, software, Siri and developer tools.

item.title: Iraq and UAE race to establish alternative oil pipelines as exports through Hormuz dry up
item.description: Last week, the Iraqi cabinet approved plans to accelerate crude exports through the Kurdistan-Turkey pipeline network.

item.title: BlackRock says 'mega forces' are changing investing. Here's what they say to do
item.description: Investors may need to rethink the most basic principles of portfolio management as AI changes the rules, according to BlackRock.

item.title: The space race is coming for pharma: Why drug development is heading to lower Earth orbit
item.description: Companies see a commercial opportunity in creating new ways to administer drugs to patients – in space.

item.title: China's May shipments to U.S. clock 5-year high growth at 35% as overall exports jump on tech boost
item.description: Exports to the U.S. surged the most since March 2021, extending a rebound following a long streak of double-digit declines for the most of last year.

item.title: Trump booed before Knicks lose to Spurs at Madison Square Garden in NBA Finals Game 3
item.description: Trump's decision to attend Game 3 of the NBA Final was controversial for Knicks fans, some of whom feared he would jinx what has been an epic playoff run.

item.title: Perplexity plans IPO in 2028 regardless of what happens to Anthropic or OpenAI, CEO tells CNBC
item.description: Perplexity CEO Aravind Srinivas' comments come after AI giant Anthropic confidentially filed to go public.

item.title: Oil prices may hit $150 per barrel soon if Iran war continues, energy economist says
Retirement 'underspending' is risky, advisor says. Here's why
item.description: Retirees often focus on the risk of spending too much in retirement. But spending too little poses another kind of danger.

item.title: He left Goldman Sachs after 26 years with no job lined up. Now he runs one of America’s oldest banks
item.description: After 26 years at Goldman Sachs, Robin Vince walked away without another job lined up. In this episode of "Executive Decisions," the BNY CEO reflects on why he stepped off a successful career path, what he learned during the global financial crisis and how that pause ultimately led him to one of America’s oldest banks.

item.title: Jim Cramer warns key pillars of the bull market are beginning to crumble
item.description: CNBC's Jim Cramer said that he's becoming more cautious on stocks after several pillars of his bullish outlook have come under pressure.

item.title: Bernstein says the future of tech is quantum. These two stocks have the most upside
item.description: While it is too early to identify the ultimate winners in quantum computing, two stocks already offer more attractive risk-reward ratios, Wall Street banks said.

item.title: Pentagon expands list of China military-linked firms to include Alibaba, Baidu in fresh blow to diplomatic thaw
item.description: The Pentagon briefly posted a similar expanded list in February, then withdrew it without explanation before Trump's China trip.

item.title: Trump nominates Todd Blanche for attorney general amid controversy over DOJ fund
item.description: Blanche previously served as a criminal defense lawyer for President Trump.

item.title: Asia chip-linked shares recover after U.S. peers bounce back
item.description: In the U.S., chip stocks powered gains on Monday, helping the S&P 500 gain 0.3%, while the tech-heavy Nasdaq Composite advanced 0.86%.

item.title: Netanyahu says war with Iran, Hezbollah isn't over after Tehran says it's halting strikes
item.description: Iran and Israel traded strikes Sunday night for the first time since the U.S. and Iran agreed to a fragile ceasefire in mid-April.

item.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: Humanoid robots are great, but they need buyers too
item.description: Chinese companies ramp up humanoid robot development and production, often with global aims.

item.title: China is helping to cushion global oil prices below $100 — but analysts warn it won’t last
item.description: China has reduced oil imports since the start of the Iran war, capping global crude prices.

item.title: Corning strikes another multibillion-dollar AI deal. What the new Amazon pact means for the stock
item.description: Amazon joins the laundry list of partnerships Corning has secured in 2026, which includes Meta Platforms and Nvidia.

item.title: Travel agents are booming. But a premium travel credit card might do the job for less
item.description: A premium travel credit card can act like a travel agent for less, offering 24/7 concierge service, booking tools and valuable travel protections.

item.title: USDA Secretary Rollins calls Texas ag chief 'unserious' amid screwworm threat
item.description: Texas Agriculture Commissioner Sid Miller lost his bid for a fourth term in a March Republican primary, despite Trump's endorsement.

item.title: Apple partnering with Google and Nvidia for most advanced AI model
item.description: Apple's artificial intelligence strategy took center stage at its Worldwide Developers Conference in Cupertino, Calif.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang declines Senate testimony on AI, China and exports
item.description: Sen. Elizabeth Warren said Nvidia’s CEO should answer questions publicly as lawmakers scrutinize AI chip sales to China and export controls.

item.title: Convicted FTX founder Sam Bankman-Fried files formal request for Trump pardon
item.description: The co-founder of the now defunct FTX crypto exchange is currently serving a 25-year federal prison sentence.

item.title: Judge blocks Trump's $100,000 H-1B visa fee
item.description: Trump had imposed the $100,000 fee in order to restrict the program, arguing that it has undermined U.S. security through the "replacement of American workers."

item.title: Progressive Nithya Raman advances to November runoff against Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass
item.description: Progressive city council member Nithya Raman has advanced to a November runoff against Los Angeles Mayor Karen Bass, setting up an unexpected matchup between two Democrats and former political allies to run the struggling city of nearly 4 million.

item.title: Tuesday's big stock stories: What’s likely to move the market in the next trading session
item.description: Semiconductor stocks made a comeback, lifting the S&P 500 and the Nasdaq Composite into positive territory Monday.

item.title: Tom Lee's clients are worried SpaceX IPO will mark a market top. He's not
item.description: The Fundstrat managing partner and head of research said that there's enough cash on the sidelines that public markets can absorb the Elon Musk company's debut.
